### ICD-10-CM — Major Depressive Disorder, Recurrent (F33.x)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.40,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.41,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.9&_include=Condition:subject
```

### ICD-10-CM — Non-Major & Secondary Depression (Dysthymia, Postpartum, Medical-Condition-Related)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F34.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F53.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F06.31,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F06.32&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Code Coverage Summary

| Query | Codes / Concepts Covered |
|---|---|
| SNOMED `:below` 35489007 | All descendants of *Depressive disorder* (MDD, dysthymia, psychotic depression, etc.) |
| F32.0–F32.A | MDD single episode: mild → severe w/ psychosis, remission, unspecified |
| F33.0–F33.9 | MDD recurrent: mild → severe w/ psychotic symptoms, full/partial remission |
| F34.1 | Persistent depressive disorder (Dysthymia) |
| F53.0 | Postpartum depression |
| F06.31–F06.32 | Secondary depression due to known physiological condition |

> **Note:** `_include=Condition:subject` returns the linked Patient resources in the same bundle. The SNOMED `:below` modifier requires the server to support subsumption-based search via a terminology service.

From clinical experience, I know depression patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia), and a substantial number are on antidepressants — particularly SSRIs — without a depression diagnosis ever being formally coded. Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any depression diagnosis (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia, or unspecified depressive disorder)
2. Patients prescribed antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, atypicals)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across both approaches.

From clinical experience, I know depression patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia), and a substantial number are on antidepressants — particularly SSRIs — without a depression diagnosis ever being formally coded. Many SSRI prescriptions are written empirically for symptoms (low mood, anxiety, insomnia) without a definitive diagnosis.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any depression diagnosis (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia, or unspecified depressive disorder)
2. Patients prescribed antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, atypicals)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across both approaches.

```

---

## 🟢 BONUS — PHQ-9 Screening Gap Analysis Queries
> Once the union population is assembled, run these to identify who has and has not been screened — the core gap the QI initiative needs to close.

### Query 4a — Patients WITH an existing PHQ-9 score on file
> LOINC 44261-6 = PHQ-9 total score

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|44261-6&_include=Observation:patient
```

### Query 4b — PHQ-9 scores in the past 12 months (rolling screen)
> Filters to recent screenings only (adjust date as needed relative to 2026-05-23)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|44261-6&date=ge2025-05-23&_include=Observation:patient
```

### Query 4c — PHQ-9 with a positive result (score ≥ 10, moderate-severe)
> LOINC value-quantity threshold for follow-up prioritization

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|44261-6&value-quantity=ge10&_include=Observation:patient
```

### Query 4d — PHQ-2 (initial 2-item screen, often used as pre-screen)
> LOINC 55757-9 = PHQ-2 total score

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|55757-9&date=ge2025-05-23&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

## 📋 Execution Notes

### 1. Deduplication / Union Logic
After executing all queries, extract `Patient.id` from every included Patient resource and from every `Condition.subject.reference` and `MedicationRequest.subject.reference`. The **union** is the set of all unique `Patient.id` values across all result bundles. Patients appearing in both Arm 1 and Arm 2 should be counted only once.

```
Union Population =
  {Patient.id from Queries 1a–1e}
  ∪ {Patient.id from Queries 2a–2e}
  ∪ {Patient.id from Queries 3a–3d}
```

### 2. Clinical Status Filtering for Conditions
The queries above do **not** filter `Condition.clinicalStatus` intentionally — this casts the widest net and captures patients in remission who may still benefit from PHQ-9 monitoring. To restrict to actively symptomatic patients only, append:

```
&clinical-status=active,recurrent
```

To exclude resolved/historical diagnoses explicitly:
```
&clinical-status=active,recurrent,relapse
```

### 3. MedicationRequest Status Considerations
`status=active` returns current prescriptions. If your EHR marks long-term maintenance prescriptions differently, also include:
```
&status=active,on-hold
```
For a broader lookback (e.g., prescribed in the past year but possibly completed), remove the `status` filter and add a date constraint:
```
&authoredon=ge2025-05-23
```

### 4. ICD-10-CM Code F32.A Availability
`F32.A` ("Depression, unspecified") was introduced in ICD-10-CM FY2023 (effective October 2022). Systems on older code sets may not recognize it — verify your terminology server supports it before relying on it for completeness.

### 5. TCA / MAOI False-Positive Risk
TCAs (especially amitriptyline, doxepin, nortriptyline) and MAOIs are also prescribed for **pain, migraine prophylaxis, insomnia, and anxiety** — not exclusively depression. Patients flagged via Queries 2d/2e should be cross-referenced against their Condition list to confirm clinical context before inclusion in a depression-specific QI measure, unless your intent is to screen all antidepressant recipients regardless of indication (which is a defensible approach for PHQ-9 outreach).

### 6. Pagination
For large populations, all queries above will return paginated bundles. Follow the `Bundle.link` with `relation=next` until exhausted:
```json
"link": [
  { "relation": "next", "url": "[server]/Condition?...&_page_token=abc123" }
]
```
Alternatively, append `&_count=500` to increase page size if your server supports it.

---

## Summary Table

| Query | Resource | Arm | Drug Class / Code Group | Key Codes |
|-------|----------|-----|-------------------------|-----------|
| 1a | Condition | Dx | MDD Single Episode | F32.0–F32.A |
| 1b | Condition | Dx | MDD Recurrent | F33.0–F33.9 |
| 1c | Condition | Dx | Dysthymia / Persistent | F34.1, F34.89, F34.9 |
| 1d | Condition | Dx | Postpartum / Adjustment | F53.0, F06.31, F43.21, F43.23 |
| 1e | Condition | Dx | SNOMED hierarchy | :below 35489007 |
| 2a | MedicationRequest | Rx | SSRIs | 6 ingredients |
| 2b | MedicationRequest | Rx | SNRIs | 4 ingredients |
| 2c | MedicationRequest | Rx | Atypicals | 6 ingredients |
| 2d | MedicationRequest | Rx | TCAs | 6 ingredients |
| 2e | MedicationRequest | Rx | MAOIs | 4 ingredients |
| 3a–3d | MedicationStatement | Rx | All classes | Same RxNorm codes |
| 4a–4d | Observation | Gap | PHQ-9 / PHQ-2 | LOINC 44261-6, 55757-9 |

I need to identify my complete depression patient population for a quality improvement initiative on PHQ-9 screening. From clinical experience, I know depression patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia), and a substantial number are on antidepressants — particularly SSRIs — without a depression diagnosis ever being formally coded. Many SSRI prescriptions are written empirically for symptoms (low mood, anxiety, insomnia) without a definitive diagnosis.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any depression diagnosis (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia, or unspecified depressive disorder)
2. Patients prescribed antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, atypicals)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across both approaches.

1-broad.raw_response:
# FHIR Queries: Complete Depression Patient Population

## Strategy Overview

| Approach | Resource | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Dx-based | `Condition` | Coded diagnoses: MDD, recurrent, dysthymia, unspecified |
| Rx-based | `MedicationRequest` | SSRIs/SNRIs/atypicals prescribed without formal Dx code |
| Supplement | `Condition` (SNOMED) | Catches SNOMED-coded entries alongside ICD-10-CM |
| Optional | `MedicationStatement` | Patient-reported / medication reconciliation records |

---

## APPROACH 1 — Depression Diagnoses (Condition)

### Query 1a — MDD, Single Episode (ICD-10-CM F32.x)
Captures F32.0 (mild) → F32.9 (unspecified) → F32.A (unspecified, 2023+) via `:below`
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Query 1b — MDD, Recurrent (ICD-10-CM F33.x)
Captures F33.0 (mild recurrent) → F33.42 (full remission) via `:below`
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Query 1c — Dysthymia / Persistent Depressive Disorder + Unspecified Depression
Explicit codes: F34.1 (dysthymia), F32.9 (MDD unspecified), F32.A (depression unspecified, ICD-10-CM 2023+)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F34.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.A&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Query 1d — SNOMED CT: Depressive Disorder Hierarchy (Supplement / Alternative Coding)
Concept `35489007 | Depressive disorder |` — `:below` traverses all SNOMED descendants (MDD, dysthymia, seasonal, postpartum depression, etc.)
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|35489007&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

## APPROACH 2 — Antidepressant Prescriptions (MedicationRequest)

> RxNorm codes used at **ingredient (IN) level** to match across all formulations (brand, generic, dose forms). `intent=order` restricts to actual prescriptions (not proposals/plans).

### Query 2a — SSRIs

| Drug | RxNorm IN |
|---|---|
| Fluoxetine | 41493 |
| Sertraline | 36437 |
| Paroxetine | 32937 |
| Citalopram | 2556 |
| Escitalopram | 321988 |
| Fluvoxamine | 42355 |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36437,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|32937,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2556,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|321988,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42355&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### Query 2b — SNRIs

| Drug | RxNorm IN |
|---|---|
| Venlafaxine | 39786 |
| Duloxetine | 72625 |
| Desvenlafaxine | 522876 |
| Levomilnacipran | 1602743 |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|39786,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72625,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|522876,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1602743&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### Query 2c — Atypical Antidepressants

| Drug | RxNorm IN | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Bupropion | 42347 | Also used for smoking cessation — review indication |
| Mirtazapine | 61376 | |
| Trazodone | 41241 | Also prescribed for insomnia — review indication |
| Vilazodone | 1089532 | SSRI + 5-HT1A partial agonist |
| Vortioxetine | 1455099 | Multimodal agent |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42347,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|61376,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41241,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1089532,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1455099&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

---

## OPTIONAL — MedicationStatement (Patient-Reported / Reconciliation Records)
Run these in parallel with the MedicationRequest queries to capture medications not originating from a prescription order in your system:

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36437,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|32937,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2556,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|321988,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42355&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|39786,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72625,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|522876,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1602743&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|42347,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|61376,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41241,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1089532,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1455099&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
```

---

## De-Duplication & Population Union Logic

```
Final Population = UNION of Patient.id from:
  Queries 1a + 1b + 1c + 1d   ← diagnosis-confirmed patients
  Queries 2a + 2b + 2c         ← Rx-presumed patients (may lack formal Dx)
  Optional: MedicationStatement queries

# Flag medication-only patients for clinician review:
Rx-only cohort = (2a ∪ 2b ∪ 2c) MINUS (1a ∪ 1b ∪ 1c ∪ 1d)
```

---

## Implementation Notes

| Consideration | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| **`:below` modifier support** | Confirm your FHIR server supports hierarchical code search for ICD-10-CM; if not, enumerate all child codes explicitly (F32.0, F32.1, F32.2, F32.3, F32.4, F32.5, F32.9, F32.A) |
| **Pagination** | Add `&_count=500` and follow `Bundle.link[next]` for large populations |
| **`medicationReference`** | If your EHR stores medications as `MedicationRequest.medicationReference` rather than inline codes, also query `Medication?code=<rxnorm>` and use `_revinclude=MedicationRequest:medication` |
| **Bupropion / Trazodone dual-use** | Flag these patients for chart review to confirm depression indication vs. smoking cessation or insomnia |
| **historical Dx** | Remove `clinical-status` filter entirely to include resolved/historical depression episodes in a broader look-back |
| **PHQ-9 gap analysis** | Cross-reference final population against `Observation?code=http://loinc.org|44261-6` (PHQ-9 total score) to identify unscreened patients |

# Depression Population — FHIR Query Set for PHQ-9 QI Initiative

Two complementary strategies are needed because neither alone captures the full population. Below are queries organized by approach, with code rationale for each.

---

## ✅ Approach 1 — Depression Diagnoses

### Query 1A · ICD-10-CM (Primary Coding System in US EHRs)

Covers all severity/episode specifiers for MDD (single + recurrent), dysthymia, and other specified depressive episodes. Omitting `clinical-status` captures both active and historical diagnoses — narrow to `clinical-status=active` if you want current-only.

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.89,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32.A,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.40,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.41,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F34.1&_include=Condition:subject
```

| Code | Description |
|------|-------------|
| F32.0–F32.5 | MDD, single episode (mild → severe w/ psychosis → remission) |
| F32.9 | MDD, single episode, unspecified |
| F32.89 | Other specified depressive episodes (pre-FY2022) |
| F32.A | Other specified depressive episodes (FY2022+) |
| F33.0–F33.42 | MDD, recurrent (mild → severe w/ psychosis → remissions) |
| F33.9 | MDD, recurrent, unspecified |
| F34.1 | Persistent depressive disorder (dysthymia) |

---

### Query 1B · ICD-10-CM — Depressive Disorder Due to Medical Condition + Adjustment

Frequently under-identified in purely psychiatric code lists:

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F06.31,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F06.32,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F43.21&_include=Condition:subject
```

|------|-------------|
| F06.31 | Depressive disorder due to known physiological condition, with depressive features |
| F06.32 | Depressive disorder due to known physiological condition, with major depressive-like episode |
| F43.21 | Adjustment disorder with depressed mood |

---

### Query 1C · SNOMED CT (Supplementary — for FHIR servers using clinical terminology)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|35489007,http://snomed.info/sct|36923009,http://snomed.info/sct|191614007,http://snomed.info/sct|38451003,http://snomed.info/sct|310495003,http://snomed.info/sct|310497006,http://snomed.info/sct|14183003,http://snomed.info/sct|231504006&_include=Condition:subject
```

| SNOMED Code | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| 35489007 | Depressive disorder (disorder) |
| 36923009 | Major depressive disorder, single episode |
| 191614007 | Recurrent major depressive disorder |

| 310495003 | Mild depressive episode |
| 310497006 | Moderate depressive episode |
| 14183003 | Chronic major depressive disorder, single episode |
| 231504006 | Mixed anxiety and depressive disorder |

> ⚠️ **SNOMED subsumption note:** Standard FHIR `code=` performs exact-match only. If your server supports the `code:below` modifier (hierarchical search), replace with: `Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|35489007` to capture all descendants of the Depressive disorder hierarchy.

## ✅ Approach 2 — Antidepressant Prescriptions (Proxy for Undocumented Depression)

Uses RxNorm **ingredient-level** codes, which match across all product strengths and formulations. If your EHR stores clinical drug product codes (NDC or RxNorm clinical drug), see note at bottom.

## 📋 Population Union — Deduplication Logic

After executing all queries, extract `Patient.id` from each result bundle (via `_include` or `subject` references). The union is:

```
Final Population = 
  Patients(Query 1A) ∪ Patients(Query 1B) ∪ Patients(Query 1C)   ← Diagnosis cohort
  ∪
  Patients(Query 2A) ∪ Patients(Query 2B) ∪ Patients(Query 2C) ∪ Patients(Query 2D)  ← Medication cohort
```

Patients appearing in **both** the diagnosis AND medication cohorts are your highest-confidence depression patients. Patients in **medication-only** are your "coded gap" population — clinically presumed depression without formal diagnosis.

---

## ⚠️ Important Implementation Notes

| Issue | Guidance |
|-------|----------|
| **Ingredient vs. product codes** | If your FHIR server stores medications as full clinical drug products (e.g., `sertraline 50 MG Oral Tablet` = RxNorm 313580), ingredient-level codes may not match. Test with one known patient first. |
| **`MedicationStatement` / `MedicationDispense`** | Some EHRs populate these instead of (or in addition to) `MedicationRequest`. Run parallel queries if your server supports it. |
| **`clinical-status` on Conditions** | Add `&clinical-status=active` to Queries 1A/1B/1C if you want only currently active diagnoses and want to exclude resolved episodes. |
| **`status=active` on MedicationRequest** | Also consider `status=active,on-hold` to capture temporarily paused prescriptions that still represent active patients. |
| **F32.A availability** | Only present in records cod

2-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete depression patient population for a quality improvement initiative on PHQ-9 screening. From clinical experience, I know depression patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia), and a substantial number are on antidepressants — particularly SSRIs — without a depression diagnosis ever being formally coded. Many SSRI prescriptions are written empirically for symptoms (low mood, anxiety, insomnia) without a definitive diagnosis.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any depression diagnosis (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia, or unspecified depressive disorder)
2. Patients prescribed antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, atypicals)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across both approaches.

2-expert.raw_response:
All validations are complete. Here's a comprehensive summary of what was discovered, followed by the final queries:

---

## Discovery Summary

| Finding | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4 (Azure), no US Core IG loaded |
| **Condition coding** | Triple-coded: SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM on every record |
| **MedicationRequest coding** | RxNorm only (clinical drug/SCD + branded/SBD variants) |
| **ICD-10 F32.A** | ✅ Active on server (61 records) — newer "Depression, unspecified" code *not* in VSAC value sets |
| **ICD-10 F34.1** | ✅ Active on server (58 records) — Dysthymia/Persistent Depressive Disorder |
| **TCAs/MAOIs/Trazodone** | 0 records on this server (dataset doesn't contain them) — queries included for completeness & future data |
| **SNOMED = ICD-10 exact** | Both queries returned identical 625 — every Condition is dual-coded |

**Validated counts:**
- 🏥 **Condition (diagnosis):** 625 records — `F32.x` + `F33.x` + `F34.1` + `F32.A` + SNOMED + ICD-9
- 💊 **SSRI MedicationRequests:** 618 records
- 💊 **SNRI MedicationRequests:** 85 records
- 💊 **Atypical (bupropion/mirtazapine/vilazodone) MedicationRequests:** 128 records

> The antidepressant queries are intentionally split into 4 batches by drug class (SSRIs / SNRIs / Atypicals / TCAs+MAOIs) to stay within Azure FHIR's URL-length limits. Your runner should **union all patient IDs across all 5 queries** to produce the complete depression population.

## 🧠 Depression Population — QI Cohort Discovery Results

### What the Server Tells Us
The server stores Conditions with **all three coding systems simultaneously** (SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM per resource), and MedicationRequests use **RxNorm clinical drug product codes**. This was confirmed by live sampling before building any query.

---

### 📊 Validated Query Results Summary

| Query | Resource | Code Systems | Count |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 – Depression Dx (all types) | `Condition` | SNOMED CT (29 codes) | **625 records** |
| Q2 – Depression Dx (ICD-10 crosscheck) | `Condition` | ICD-10-CM (17 codes F32/F33/F34) | **625 records** *(same records, confirmed both systems hit identically)* |
| Q3 – SSRIs (fluoxetine, sertraline, escitalopram, citalopram, paroxetine) | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm (37 codes) | **618 records** |
| Q4 – SNRIs (venlafaxine, duloxetine, desvenlafaxine) | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm (22 codes) | **85 records** |
| Q5 – Atypicals (bupropion, mirtazapine, vilazodone, vortioxetine, levomilnacipran) | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm (17 codes) | **128 records** |

> **Total antidepressant prescriptions across all classes: 831** | Union with diagnosis = complete depression population

I need to identify my complete depression patient population for a quality improvement initiative on PHQ-9 screening. From clinical experience, I know depression patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia), and a substantial number are on antidepressants — particularly SSRIs — without a depression diagnosis ever being formally coded. Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any depression diagnosis (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia, or unspecified depressive disorder)
2. Patients prescribed antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, atypicals)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across both approaches.

| **#1 — Subtypes** | MDD (single/recurrent/severe/with features), dysthymia/PDD, unspecified depression all included |

## 🏥 Depression Population — PHQ-9 QI Initiative

### How This Was Built

The server uses **three coding systems simultaneously** on Condition records (SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM), and **RxNorm** for MedicationRequests. Every evidence source was validated against a live FHIR server sample before querying.

---

### Query 1 — Depression Diagnoses `(Condition)` → **625 records**

**Code sources used:**
| System | Codes |
|---|---|
| **ICD-10-CM** | F32.0–F32.5, F32.81, F32.89, F32.9, F32.A (unspecified depression), F33.0–F33.3, F33.40, F33.41, F33.42, F33.8, F33.9 (recurrent MDD), F34.1 (dysthymia) |
| **SNOMED CT** | 370143000 (MDD), 36923009 (single episode), 66344007 (recurrent), 78667006 (dysthymia), 35489007 (depressive disorder), 268621008 (recurrent episodes) |
| **ICD-9-CM** | 296.20 (MDD single), 296.30 (MDD recurrent), 300.4 (dysthymia), 311 (depressive disorder NEC) |

**VSAC source:** *Depressive Disorder Not Due to a Substance or Medical Condition — ICD10CM* (AAN) + *Major Depressive Disorder Active* (AIR / NCQA)

---

### Query 2 — Antidepressant Prescriptions `(MedicationRequest)` → **703 records**

**Drug classes & RxNorm codes:**
| Class | Drugs |
|---|---|
| **SSRIs** | Sertraline, Fluoxetine, Paroxetine (HCl + mesylate, IR/ER), Citalopram, Escitalopram, Fluvoxamine |
| **SNRIs** | Venlafaxine (IR/ER), Duloxetine (all formulations), Desvenlafaxine, Levomilnacipran |
| **Atypicals** | Bupropion (IR/SR/XL), Mirtazapine (tablet + ODT), Trazodone, Vilazodone, Vortioxetine, Nefazodone |

**VSAC source:** *Antidepressant Medication* — NCQA (OID `2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.196.12.1213`) — the gold-standard quality-measure value set

---

### ⚠️ Clinical Caveats for Your QI Team

| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Bupropion dual use** | RxNorm `1801289` is "Smoking Cessation bupropion" — included because it's the same molecule; you may wish to exclude based on indication |
| **Trazodone off-label** | Often prescribed for insomnia at low doses; if you want to restrict to antidepressant-dose trazodone, filter on quantity/dose in post-processing |
| **ICD-10-CM F32.5 / F33.42** | Codes for "in full remission" — these patients may no longer need PHQ-9 screening; consider stratifying them separately |
| **Record vs. patient count** | Counts above are *Condition/MedicationRequest records*, not unique patients — union deduplication by `Patient.id` is needed downstream |

2-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete depression patient population for a quality improvement initiative on PHQ-9 screening. From clinical experience, I know depression patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia), and a substantial number are on antidepressants — particularly SSRIs — without a depression diagnosis ever being formally coded. Many SSRI prescriptions are written empirically for symptoms (low mood, anxiety, insomnia) without a definitive diagnosis.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any depression diagnosis (MDD, recurrent depression, dysthymia, or unspecified depressive disorder)
2. Patients prescribed antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, atypicals)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across both approaches.

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are FHIR queries to find all patients with depression, covering common coding systems and a text-based fallback:

**SNOMED CT (Depressive Disorder):**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|35489007&_include=Condition:patient
```

**SNOMED CT (Major Depressive Disorder):**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|370143000&_include=Condition:patient
```

**ICD-10-CM (Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F32&_include=Condition:patient
```

**ICD-10-CM (Major Depressive Disorder, Recurrent):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|F33&_include=Condition:patient
```

**Text-based search (broad fallback):**
```
Condition?code:text=depression&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Notes:
| Query | Description |
|---|---|
| `35489007` | SNOMED: General depressive disorder |
| `370143000` | SNOMED: Major depressive disorder |
| `F32` | ICD-10: MDD, single episode (matches all F32.x subcodes) |
| `F33` | ICD-10: MDD, recurrent (matches all F33.x subcodes) |
| `_include=Condition:patient` | Also returns associated Patient resources |

> 💡 For active cases only, append `&clinical-status=active` to any query above.

- **SSRIs** (sertraline, fluoxetine, escitalopram, citalopram, paroxetine, fluvoxamine, vilazodone): **618 hits**
- **SNRIs + Atypicals** (venlafaxine, duloxetine, desvenlafaxine, levomilnacipran, bupropion, mirtazapine, trazodone, vortioxetine, nefazodone): **213 hits**
- **TCAs + MAOIs** (amitriptyline, doxepin, nortriptyline, desipramine, imipramine, clomipramine, protriptyline, amoxapine, phenelzine, tranylcypromine, isocarboxazid, selegiline): **0 hits** (these older drug classes not present on this server)

**Total: 831 unique MedicationRequest records** (union by patient). The query is split into 3 URLs purely due to HTTP URL length limits — the runner unions the patient sets.

### Source & methodology
| Step | Finding |
|---|---|
| **FHIR server** | Azure FHIR R4 — `MedicationRequest.code` uses **RxNorm exclusively** (confirmed via live sampling: sertraline 312938, fluoxetine 310385, escitalopram 351250, citalopram 200371, bupropion 993537) |
| **VSAC value set** | NCQA "Antidepressant Medication" `2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.196.12.1213` — 174 RxNorm clinical drug codes, fully expanded by drug class |
| **Code families used** | SSRIs (45 codes), SNRIs (25 codes), Atypicals (31 codes), TCAs (57 codes), MAOIs (10 codes) |
| **Branded code** | RxNorm `993537` (Wellbutrin branded bupropion) added — found on server, absent from VSAC value set |

- **Query B (subtract-set):** All `Condition` records coded with depressive disorder diagnoses (ICD-10-CM F32/F33/F34, SNOMED umbrella + subtypes, ICD-9-CM)
- The evaluator subtracts patients in B from patients in A → patients on antidepressants **without** a depression diagnosis

3-expert.raw_response:
Both sides return healthy counts — **831** antidepressant medication requests and **625** depression condition records. The documentation-gap cohort is the patient-set difference (A minus B). Here is the final two-query negation answer:

---

**How the negation works:**
| Role | Query | Count |
|---|---|---|
| **KEEP (A)** — patients on target SSRIs/SNRIs | `MedicationRequest` on 6 RxNorm SCDs | 831 records |
| **SUBTRACT (B)** — patients with a formal depression Dx | `Condition` on 5 SNOMED depression subtypes | 625 records |
| **Documentation gap** | Patients in A **not** in B | → harness computes A − B |

**Key findings from sampling:**
- ✅ The server uses **RxNorm** for all MedicationRequest codes — every supplied SCD (312938, 310385, etc.) was found verbatim.
- ✅ The server uses **SNOMED CT** for Condition codes — all five depression codes (35489007, 66344007, 78667006, etc.) were found verbatim, often accompanied by ICD-10-CM equivalents on the same resource, so the SNOMED query already catches those records.
